EX 34 – Expiry Prompt Suppression MCLN leverages Recall Inventory Rules to recall any inventory and locate the inventory to a reserve location set on the rules. The recall inventory flow uses Pck iLPN from Active or Pck iLPN from Case Pick transactions to pull the inventory out of the pick locations. EX34 is enhanced to suppress the expiry prompt that is displayed after scanning the quantity in the Pck iLPN from Active or Pck iLPN from Case Pick flow even if the item recalled is an expiry tracked item. Summary Information Customer Name: McLane Customer Code: MCLN Subproject Codes: TBD Change Request Number: Overview Functional Area Enhanced Additional Information User Interface (UI) N MHE N RF Y Reporting N MCLN uses Pck iLPN from CP/Active transaction to pull inventory out of pick locations during Recall. With EX34, WMOS is enhance d to suppress the expiry prompt for the base Pck iLPN from CP and Pck iLPN from Act flow s . Assumptions MCLN uses Pck iLPN from CP or Pck iLPN from Active to recall active inventory Recalling iLPNs from reserve is out of scope for this extension Dependencies Code Dependencies N/A Interface Dependencies Interface Field Requirement P latform Information Solution: Solution Platform: Solution Version: Warehouse Management Open Systems Oracle/ Tomcat /RHEL 201 9 Proces s Flow Diagram UI No UI changes in scope for this extension. MHE No MHE changes in scope for this extension. RF Pck iLPN from CP and Pck iLPN from Active Per base WM, Pck iLPN from CP/Active transaction checks if the item being packed out is expiry tracked or not (ITEM_WMS.XPIRE_DATE_REQD) . If the item is set to “Always Prompt’ or ‘Prompt if null’ , WM will display the following screen after scanning the item quantity. EX34 is enhanced to suppress the following screen after scanning the quantity if the item is expiry tracked . Essentially with EX34 enabled, a user will never be prompted for the Expire Date prompt. Process Change/Addition Reporting , Label, or Document Not report, label, or document changes in scope for this extension . Other Solutions Included in Extension Not in scope for this extension. Performance Expectations The p urpose of this section is to identify performance requirements up front Process Step Peak Transaction Rate Max Response 95% Response Recovery Queue Recovery Time Operations Driver & notes Required Data Setup All necessary data setup for the extension to function accurately are detailed below.
